title: QQ / DingTalk / WeCom Bot Message Flow
interaction_flow: QQApp: dedup check via PROCESSED_IDS deque(maxlen=1000)
QQApp: extract content, user_id, chat_id (group_openid or user_openid)
QQApp [if cmd]: → AgentChatMixin.handle_command(chat_id, content, msg_id, is_group)
QQApp [if msg]: asyncio.create_task → AgentChatMixin.run_agent(chat_id, content, msg_id, is_group)
AgentChatMixin.run_agent → GeneraticAgent.put_task(text, source='qq') → returns deque dq
AgentChatMixin.run_agent: poll dq via asyncio.to_thread(dq.get, True, 3)
DingTalkApp.on_message: chat_id = 'group:{conversationId}' if group else sender_id
DingTalkApp [if msg]: asyncio.create_task → run_agent(chat_id, content) [tracked in background_tasks set]
WeComApp.on_text: extract body from frame.body or frame dict
WeComApp.on_text: dedup via PROCESSED_IDS (msgid or chatid+sendertime key)
WeComApp.send_text → WSClient.reply_stream(frame, req_id, part, finish=True) [split chunks]
run_agent: poll dq every 3s via asyncio.to_thread
run_agent [Q.Empty + is_running + ping_interval exceeded]: send '⏳ 还在处理中...' ping
key_relationships: QQBot (inner class) wraps QQApp via closure — delegates all events to app.on_message()
_DingTalkHandler extends CallbackHandler — holds DingTalkApp ref, calls app.on_message()
AgentChatMixin holds GeneraticAgent ref (shared singleton per process) and USER_TASKS dict
QQBot uses _build_intents() to configure botpy.Intents (public_messages, direct_message, group_at_message etc.)
DingTalkApp.start() registers _DingTalkHandler on ChatbotMessage.TOPIC via DingTalkStreamClient
WeComApp.start() registers event handlers on WSClient via .on(event, handler)
GeneraticAgent.put_task() returns a deque (dq) used as async result queue
GeneraticAgent.run() is launched in daemon thread; agent is shared across all chat_ids
auth_and_dedup: All three apps use ALLOWED set (from mykeys) + public_access() for authorization
QQ & WeCom: PROCESSED_IDS = deque(maxlen=1000) for msg dedup by msg_id
DingTalk: no explicit dedup (stream guarantees delivery once)
DingTalk: CLIENT_ID/CLIENT_SECRET; OAuth2 access token cached until expiry-60s
WeCom: BOT_ID/SECRET; WSClient handles reconnect (max_reconnect_attempts=-1, heartbeat=30s)
send_text_mechanics: QQ: split_text(content, 1500) → post_group_message(group_openid) or post_c2c_message(openid) with msg_seq (thread-safe counter)
DingTalk: split_text(content, 1800) → _send_batch_message → sampleMarkdown REST API (group or 1:1)
WeCom: split_text(content, 1200) → WSClient.reply_stream(frame, req_id, part, finish=True)
important_notes: All three frontends follow identical pattern: platform SDK event → on_message/on_text → auth check → dedup → command or agent task → run_agent polls GeneraticAgent deque → send_text response. QQBot is dynamically created inner class (_make_bot_class) so it captures QQApp instance. DingTalk adds OAuth2 token management. WeCom stores frame per chat_id for reply context. All agents run in same process with shared GeneraticAgent singleton on daemon thread.
